title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problem_statement: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree where the path may start and end at any node.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4]
intuition: Use recursion to compute maximum gain from each subtree and update a global max path sum.

approaches.name: Post-order recursion with global max
approaches.description: Compute the maximum path sum that extends upward from each node while tracking the best path through the node.
approaches.why_not: Trying to compute all paths explicitly is exponential in the worst case.

interview_qa.a: Because a path can stop before a negative subtree and avoid reducing the sum.

interview_qa.q: What does the recursive helper return?
interview_qa.a: The max path sum from the current node to any leaf in one direction.
